scheme.name: Delhi Arogya Kosh (DAK)
scheme.department: Department of Health & Family Welfare, Government of NCT of Delhi (GNCTD)
scheme.department_hindi: स्वास्थ्य एवं परिवार कल्याण विभाग, दिल्ली सरकार
scheme.description: Delhi Arogya Kosh (DAK) is a Registered Society under the Government of NCT of Delhi that provides financial assistance up to ₹5 Lakhs to eligible needy patients for treatment of any illness or disease in Government Hospitals. The scheme covers treatment in hospitals run by the Delhi Government, Central Government, Local Bodies, and Autonomous Hospitals under the State Government (including AIIMS). Eligible patients must hold a National Food Security Card and must be bonafide residents of Delhi for the last 3 years prior to application submission. Applications must be submitted in person at the Patient Welfare Cell, Directorate of Health Services, Karkardooma, Delhi. Financial assistance is disbursed as a cheque issued directly to the concerned Government Hospital on behalf of the approved patient. The scheme follows a multi-level approval process through the Director Health Services, Finance Department (GNCTD), and the Secretary (Health) & Chairman DAK.
scheme.description_hindi: दिल्ली आरोग्य कोष (डीएके) दिल्ली सरकार के अंतर्गत एक पंजीकृत सोसाइटी है जो पात्र जरूरतमंद रोगियों को सरकारी अस्पतालों में किसी भी बीमारी के इलाज के लिए ₹5 लाख तक की वित्तीय सहायता प्रदान करती है। यह योजना दिल्ली सरकार, केंद्र सरकार, स्थानीय निकायों और राज्य सरकार के स्वायत्त अस्पतालों (एम्स सहित) द्वारा संचालित अस्पतालों में उपचार को कवर करती है। पात्र रोगियों के पास राष्ट्रीय खाद्य सुरक्षा कार्ड होना चाहिए और आवेदन जमा करने से पहले पिछले 3 वर्षों से दिल्ली का वास्तविक निवासी होना चाहिए। आवेदन व्यक्तिगत रूप से रोगी कल्याण प्रकोष्ठ, स्वास्थ्य सेवा निदेशालय, करकरडूमा, दिल्ली में जमा करना होता है। वित्तीय सहायता सीधे संबंधित सरकारी अस्पताल को चेक के रूप में जारी की जाती है।
scheme.benefits_summary: Financial assistance of up to ₹5,00,000 (Five Lakhs) per eligible patient for treatment of any illness/disease in Government Hospitals. The cheque is issued directly to the concerned Government Hospital (not to the patient). The scheme covers all types of treatments including operations, cyclical treatments (chemotherapy, dialysis etc.), and any other medical expenditure as certified by the treating doctor and Medical Superintendent of the concerned Government Hospital.

eligibility.domicile_duration: 3 years (bonafide resident of Delhi for the last 3 years prior to application submission)
eligibility.beneficiary_family_definition: Spouse and children up to 21 years of age, as declared in the undertaking/affidavit (Part C of the application form).
eligibility.government_employee_exclusion: Applicant and their parents must NOT be employees of the Central Government, State Government, or a Local Body.
other_conditions: Patient must hold a valid National Food Security Card.
Patient must be a bonafide resident of Delhi for the last 3 years prior to application submission.
Treatment must be required in a Government Hospital run by Delhi Govt., Central Govt., AIIMS, Autonomous Institutes of State Govt., or Local Bodies.
Family annual income must be up to ₹3,00,000.
Neither the applicant nor their parents should be employees of the Central/State Government or a Local Body.
Patient must be unable to arrange or provide funds for the treatment (as declared in the application).
For minor patients: birth certificate of the patient AND domicile proof of either parent is required.
Previous assistance from any other source or from Delhi Arogya Nidhi/Kosh must be disclosed in the application.
All information furnished must be true; false statements are punishable under relevant Acts and laws.

application_steps: Step 1: Obtain the Application Form (Parts A, B, and C) from the Patient Welfare Cell at Room No. 1, 6th Floor, Directorate of Health Services, F-17 Karkardooma, Delhi-110032, or download from health.delhi.gov.in.

Step 2: Get Part B (Estimate Certificate) filled and signed by the treating doctor of the concerned Government Hospital, with certification by the Head of Department and Medical Superintendent with official seal.
Step 3: Get two passport-size photographs of the patient attested by the Medical Superintendent or treating doctor/consultant. Paste one on the Estimate Certificate and one on the Application Form.
Step 4: Obtain Original Monthly Income Certificate from all sources issued by area SDM or authorized Revenue Department officer.
Step 5: Collect all required domicile proof documents, treatment records, OPD slip copy, and National Food Security Card photocopy.
Step 6: Complete Part A (Patient Information) of the Application Form with all required details and correct signature as per dependency status.
Step 7: Complete Part C (Undertaking/Affidavit) with deponent declaration, family details, two witness signatures, and verification.

Step 8: Submit the complete application with all documents IN PERSON at the Patient Welfare Cell, Room No. 1, 6th Floor, DHS, F-17 Karkardooma, Delhi-110032. Carry all ORIGINAL documents for verification.

Step 9: Application is processed through: Patient Welfare Cell → Director Health Services (Approval) → Finance Department GNCTD (Approval) → Secretary Health & Chairman DAK (Approval) → Patient Welfare Cell.
Step 10: Upon approval, cheque is issued directly to the concerned Government Hospital. Applicant is informed via letter. Utilization certificate must be submitted after treatment is over.
scheme.offline_process: Application must be submitted IN PERSON at the Patient Welfare Cell, Room No. 1, 6th Floor, Directorate of Health Services, F-17 Karkardooma (Near Karkardooma Court), Delhi-110032, Tel: 011-22306851. No online application facility is available. The patient or their representative must visit with all original documents for verification. Application forms can also be downloaded from the official Delhi Health & Family Welfare website.
scheme.processing_time: In person; processing begins upon receipt of complete application with all required documents.

helpline.address: Office of Patient Welfare Cell, Room No. 1, 6th Floor, Directorate of Health Services, F-17 Karkardooma (Near Karkardooma Court), Delhi-110032
helpline.department_address: Department of Health & Family Welfare, 9th Level, A-Wing, IP Extension, Delhi Secretariat, Delhi-110002

benefits_amount_detail.disbursement_mode: Cheque issued directly to the concerned Government Hospital
benefits_amount_detail.coverage_scope: Treatment of any illness/disease including operations, cyclical treatments (chemotherapy/dialysis), and other medical expenditure

benefits_frequency_detail.disbursement: Cheque released after multi-level approval (Director Health Services → Finance Department GNCTD → Secretary Health & Chairman DAK)
benefits_frequency_detail.repeat_applications: Prior DAK/Delhi Arogya Nidhi assistance must be disclosed in application; repeat assistance subject to review

processing_time_detail.application_submission: In person; processing begins upon receipt of complete application with all required documents.
processing_time_detail.approval_chain: Multi-level: Patient Welfare Cell → Director Health Services → Finance Department GNCTD → Secretary Health & Chairman DAK → Patient Welfare Cell
processing_time_detail.disbursement: Cheque issued to concerned Government Hospital after final approval; applicant informed via letter.
processing_time_detail.utilization_certificate: Must be submitted after treatment is over if grants were released.

documents_required_detailed: Photocopy of National Food Security Card (carry original for verification at time of submission).
Domicile proof for the last 3 years – any one of: Domicile Certificate issued by area SDM, Ration Card, EPIC (Voter ID), Driving License, Passport, Extract from Electoral Roll, or Aadhaar Card.
For minor patients: Birth Certificate of the patient AND domicile proof of either parent (any one document from the approved domicile list).

Original Estimate Certificate (Part B of the form) issued by the treating doctor of the concerned Government Hospital, indicating patient's disease, treatment required, and estimated expenditure – duly certified by the Head of Department and Medical Superintendent with official seal.

Two passport-size photographs of the patient – duly attested by the Medical Superintendent or treating doctor/consultant of the concerned Government Hospital. One photograph must be pasted on the Estimate Certificate and one on the Application Form.
Photocopies of treatment records from the Government Hospital.
Copy of OPD slip from the Government Hospital.
Original Monthly Income Certificate from all sources, issued by SDM or authorized Revenue Department officer.

Completed Application Form (Part A) with correct signature as per patient dependency status (minor → father, housewife → husband, adult self-dependent → patient, adult dependent on father → father + affidavit of dependency).
Completed Estimate Certificate (Part B) with all three required signatures: Treating Doctor, Head of Department, and Medical Superintendent with official seal.
Signed Undertaking/Affidavit (Part C) with deponent's details, family income declaration, family details (spouse and children ≤21 years), and two witnesses.
Details of any prior assistance received from any other source or from Delhi Arogya Nidhi/Kosh (if applicable).
notes: The application form has three parts: Part A (Patient Information), Part B (Estimate Certificate filled by treating doctor), and Part C (Undertaking & Affidavit).
All original documents must be carried for verification at the time of submission.
Cheque is issued directly to the Government Hospital, NOT to the patient.
Utilization certificate of grants released must be submitted after the treatment is over.
False statements in the application are punishable under relevant Acts and laws.

documents.name: Income Certificate
format_requirements: Must be on official letterhead with office seal
Should contain financial year mentioned
Must have officer's signature and designation
QR code for verification (if issued after 2023)
Only PDF file, size up to 100kb (for PMAY-U 2.0 upload)
Must be ORIGINAL (not photocopy)
Must state monthly income from ALL sources
Must be issued by SDM or authorized Revenue Department officer
Must be on official letterhead with officer's signature, designation, and official seal
Family annual income must not exceed ₹3,00,000
Must be issued by the SDM of the concerned area
Should establish that annual income of parents/guardian is below ₹5,00,000/-
Official letterhead with office seal and SDM signature
Alternative: Form No. 16 of Co-Borrower from employer
common_mistakes: Income figure doesn't match salary slips
Certificate older than 6 months
Missing SDM/Tehsildar signature
Wrong income head mentioned (agricultural vs non-agricultural)
File size exceeds 100kb PDF limit when uploading to portal
Submitting photocopy instead of original income certificate
Income certificate older than 6 months
Income from all sources not fully declared
Missing SDM/authorized officer's signature or seal
Certificate showing annual income exceeding ₹3,00,000
Income certificate expired (older than 6 months)
Income figure exceeds ₹5,00,000/- threshold
Certificate issued by incorrect SDM jurisdiction
Form 16 not reflecting total family income from all sources

documents.name: Domicile Certificate
format_requirements: Proof of residence for minimum 3 years
School leaving certificate or voter ID showing Delhi address
Must be on official letterhead
Officer signature and seal mandatory
Any ONE of the following is accepted: Domicile Certificate issued by area SDM, Ration Card, EPIC (Voter ID), Driving License, Passport, Extract from Electoral Roll, or Aadhaar Card
Document must clearly establish Delhi residency for the last 3 years
Must carry ORIGINAL at the time of application submission for verification
For minor patients: Birth Certificate of the patient AND domicile proof of either parent required

Any one or more of the following is accepted: Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Ration Card, Driving License, Aadhaar Card, Passport, Insurance Policy Documents, Utility Bills (Electricity/Water/Telephone/Gas Connection receipt), Bank Passbook (with same address), Property Documents, Rent Agreements (covering last 5 years), or any other document clearly showing 5+ years residence in Delhi
Document(s) must collectively establish continuous 5-year residency in Delhi prior to the date of application
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal

If documentary proof is unavailable: statements of any 2 witnesses from the approved list must be submitted before the District WCD Officer. Witnesses must provide photo ID, residence proof, contact details, and length of stay in the area. Home verification may be conducted.
common_mistakes: Residence proof doesn't cover required duration
Address mismatch between Aadhaar and proof documents
Rent agreement not notarized
Utility bills in different person's name
Document does not cover the full 3-year residency period
Address on domicile proof does not match current Delhi residence
Not carrying original document for verification (only photocopy submitted)
For minors: submitting only the child's domicile proof without parent's proof
Using expired or cancelled Voter ID / Driving License
Documents do not cover the full 5-year continuous residency period
Address on residence proof does not match the address mentioned in the application
Utility bills or rent agreements not covering the required 5-year period
Witness statements not from the approved list of witness categories
Witnesses unable to provide photo ID, residence proof, or contact details

documents.name: Birth Certificate
format_requirements: Must contain child's full name as registered
Parents' names must be complete
Place of birth (hospital name or address)
Registration number and date
Registrar's signature and seal
Required ONLY for minor patients (below 18 years)
Must clearly state the child's name, date of birth, and parents' names
Must be issued by competent authority (Municipal Corporation / Registrar)
Must be accompanied by domicile proof of either parent

Any ONE of the following is accepted: Birth Certificate (MCD/Registrar of Births & Deaths), School Leaving Certificate (last class attended), Matriculation/10th Class Certificate, Hospital Discharge Slip at birth, Voter ID Card, PAN Card, Driving License, Aadhaar Card, Passport, Age Assessment Medical Certificate, or any Govt./Govt.-recognized document stating date and place of birth
Must clearly state the applicant's date of birth or age
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
Applicant must be 18 years or above as per the age proof document
Copy of Birth Certificate or valid Age Proof document
Must establish the date of birth of the applicant
Self-attested copy required
common_mistakes: Late registration without affidavit
Name spelling inconsistent with school records
Missing parents' details
Hospital discharge summary not attached (for late registration)
Not submitting birth certificate for minor patients
Birth certificate not accompanied by parent's domicile proof
Name or date of birth mismatch between birth certificate and other documents
Using hospital discharge summary instead of official birth certificate
Date of birth on age proof does not match Aadhaar or other submitted documents
Age proof document does not clearly state the date of birth
Applicant is below 18 years of age as per the document submitted
Using an unofficial or unrecognized document as age proof
Date of birth mismatch with other submitted documents
Document not self-attested

documents.name: Self-Declaration / Affidavit
format_requirements: Must state that beneficiary does not own a pucca house anywhere in India
Must declare no benefits availed from any Central/State/Local housing scheme in last 20 years
Must be signed by the applicant
Notarization or attestation by competent authority where required by State/UT
Must declare: patient's disease, hospital where treatment is being done, approximate expenditure as certified by hospital
Must declare total family monthly income with specific source details
Must include family details table: spouse and children up to 21 years (name, age, relation, profession, monthly income)
Must declare inability to bear treatment expenses
Must acknowledge that false statements are punishable under relevant Acts and laws
Must include verification clause with date and place (New Delhi)
Must be signed by TWO witnesses with their names and addresses

Deponent rules: Minor patient → Father signs; Housewife patient → Husband signs; Adult self-dependent → Patient signs; Adult dependent on father → Father signs (+ separate affidavit of dependency required)
Must be in the format available on the e-District portal
Must declare annual income from all sources (including rent, interest, dividends, farm earnings, property sale proceeds, etc.)
Annual income declared must be less than ₹1,00,000
No recommendation or attestation from any external authority is required; only self-declaration by applicant
Must be self-attested, scanned, and uploaded on e-District portal
Must be in the prescribed format (proforma attached with the RGSRY application form)
Part D: Declaration of scheme compliance and document authenticity
Part E: Undertaking regarding previous loan status – must select Option (a) 'No Previous Loan' or Option (b) 'Previous Loan Details' with full disclosure of outstanding amounts
Part E must include: date of birth, education qualification, cooperative society membership status, and final affirmation of truthfulness
Must include self-attested photograph pasted with half signature on photograph and half on paper

Must declare that neither the applicant, spouse, nor any family member including adult children have taken or applied for loans from any of the listed 9 categories of institutions (unless fully repaid)
Must declare that no family member is a member of any Co-operative Society/Registered Institution that has applied for or taken loan from the listed departments
Affidavit stating that no loan has been availed from any Government Agency for the same purpose
Must state that no member of the family or dependent has availed the loan facility under the scheme of this Corporation, Government of India, or any other financial institution for the said purpose
Must be notarized or attested as applicable
common_mistakes: Declaration not covering all family members (husband, wife, unmarried children)
Missing notary stamp or seal
Not declaring previous housing benefits availed
Incorrect factual statements leading to disqualification
Wrong deponent signing (e.g., patient signing when they are a dependent minor)
Missing witness signatures or incomplete witness details
Family income details not matching the income certificate
Not disclosing all sources of income
Missing affidavit of dependency when adult patient is dependent on father
Incomplete family details table
Not acknowledging the penal consequences of false statements
Not using the prescribed format available on the e-District portal
Declaring income that exceeds the ₹1,00,000 annual limit
Not disclosing all sources of income (rent, interest, dividends, farm earnings, property sale proceeds, etc.)
False income declaration – punishable with penal action, penalty, pension stoppage, and recovery of amount
Not using the prescribed proforma format
Checking Option (a) 'No Previous Loan' when a previous loan exists
Not disclosing spouse's or adult children's loans from listed institutions
Missing self-attested photograph with half-signature placement
Not acknowledging penal consequences of false information
Concealing cooperative society membership
Not covering all family members in the declaration
Previously availed loan from another government scheme for the same purpose not disclosed

documents.name: National Food Security Card
documents.issuing_authority: Department of Food, Supplies and Consumer Affairs, GNCTD
documents.validity_period: Until cancelled or updated by issuing authority
format_requirements: Must contain names of all eligible family members
Must have valid card number
Address must be within NCT of Delhi
Must be current and not expired/cancelled
common_mistakes: Card not updated after change of address within Delhi
Family member names missing or spelled incorrectly
Card cancelled due to non-collection of rations for extended period
Carrying photocopy without original for verification

documents.name: Estimate Certificate (Part B of DAK Form)
documents.name_hindi: अनुमानित व्यय प्रमाण पत्र (डीएके फॉर्म का भाग बी)
documents.issuing_authority: Treating Doctor of the concerned Government Hospital
documents.validity_period: Valid for the current application/treatment period
format_requirements: Must be filled by the treating doctor of the concerned Government Hospital
Must contain: patient's name, age, hospital name, OPD/Registration No., father/husband's name, address, diagnosis of disease, and financial assistance required

Must include treatment cost breakdown: (a) operation details and estimated expenditure, (b) cyclical treatment details (chemotherapy/dialysis) with per-month or per-cycle cost and item-wise expenditure, (c) any other expenditure details
Must be signed by THREE authorities: (1) Treating Doctor, (2) Head of Department, (3) Medical Superintendent with Official Seal
One passport-size photograph of the patient must be pasted on the Estimate Certificate, attested by the treating doctor
Must be submitted in ORIGINAL (not photocopy)
common_mistakes: Missing signature of Head of Department or Medical Superintendent
Missing official seal of Medical Superintendent
Photograph not attested by treating doctor
Incomplete treatment cost breakdown (missing item-wise expenditure for cyclical treatments)
Estimate Certificate not filled by the treating doctor (filled by patient instead)
Submitting photocopy instead of original

documents.name: Attested Passport-Size Photographs of Patient
documents.name_hindi: रोगी के सत्यापित पासपोर्ट आकार के फोटो
documents.issuing_authority: Any photo studio (attestation by Medical Superintendent / Treating Doctor / Consultant of Government Hospital)
documents.processing_time: Instant (photographs); attestation depends on hospital availability
format_requirements: Two passport-size photographs of the patient required
Must be duly attested by the Medical Superintendent or treating doctor/consultant of the concerned Government Hospital
One photograph must be pasted on the Estimate Certificate (Part B)
One photograph must be pasted on the Application Form (Part A)
One passport-size photograph of the applicant
Must be recent and clearly identifiable
Must be scanned and uploaded on the e-District portal during application
Two passport-size photographs required
Must be attested
One photograph to be pasted on the self-declaration (Part E) with half signature on photograph and half on paper
Four passport-size photographs each of the Borrower, Co-Borrower, and Guarantor
One full-size photograph of the Borrower if the applicant belongs to PwD category
Photographs should be recent and clearly identifiable
common_mistakes: Photographs not attested by hospital authority
Only one photograph submitted instead of two
Photographs pasted on wrong form sections
Photographs not recent or not clearly identifiable
Photograph too old or not clearly identifiable
Photograph not in passport-size format
Blurred or low-resolution scan uploaded on the portal
Photographs not attested
Self-attested photograph on Part E not done correctly (half-signature placement)
Old or unclear photographs
Insufficient number of photographs (need 4 each for borrower, co-borrower, and guarantor)
Missing full-size photograph for PwD category applicant

documents.name: Treatment Records (Photocopies)
documents.processing_time: Instant to 1-2 days (depends on hospital records department)
documents.validity_period: Must be current treatment records relevant to the application
format_requirements: Photocopies of all treatment records from the Government Hospital
Must include diagnosis, treatment history, and ongoing treatment details
Records must be from a Government Hospital (Delhi Govt., Central Govt., AIIMS, Autonomous Institutes, or Local Bodies)
common_mistakes: Submitting treatment records from private hospitals (not eligible)
Incomplete records missing diagnosis or treatment details
Records not matching the disease mentioned in the Estimate Certificate

documents.name: OPD Slip
documents.issuing_authority: Outpatient Department of the concerned Government Hospital
format_requirements: Must be from the Government Hospital where treatment is being sought
Must contain patient's name, registration number, department, and date of visit
Photocopy to be attached with the application
common_mistakes: OPD slip from a different hospital than the one mentioned in the Estimate Certificate
Illegible or damaged OPD slip
OPD slip not matching the patient's name in the application

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the patient does not hold a valid National Food Security Card. This is a mandatory eligibility criterion for the Delhi Arogya Kosh scheme.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि रोगी के पास वैध राष्ट्रीय खाद्य सुरक्षा कार्ड नहीं है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। यह दिल्ली आरोग्य कोष योजना के लिए अनिवार्य पात्रता मानदंड है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Ensure the patient has a valid, current National Food Security Card before applying. If the card is expired or cancelled, get it renewed from the local Food & Supply Office.
examples: Patient does not possess any National Food Security Card
National Food Security Card has been cancelled due to non-collection of rations
Patient's name not listed on the family's National Food Security Card

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the patient cannot prove bonafide residency of Delhi for the last 3 years prior to application submission. At least one valid domicile proof document from the approved list must be provided.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि रोगी आवेदन जमा करने से पहले पिछले 3 वर्षों से दिल्ली का वास्तविक निवास प्रमाणित नहीं कर पाता है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। अनुमोदित सूची में से कम से कम एक वैध अधिवास प्रमाण दस्तावेज प्रदान करना होगा।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Carry any one valid domicile proof (Domicile Certificate from SDM, Ration Card, Voter ID, Driving License, Passport, Electoral Roll extract, or Aadhaar Card) showing Delhi address for the last 3 years. For minors, additionally carry birth certificate and parent's domicile proof.
examples: Patient has been residing in Delhi for only 2 years
Domicile proof document shows address outside Delhi
Document does not cover the required 3-year period
Minor patient's application submitted without parent's domicile proof

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the treatment is sought or being received at a private hospital. DAK covers only treatment in Government Hospitals run by Delhi Govt., Central Govt., AIIMS, Autonomous Institutes of State Govt., or Local Bodies.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि उपचार किसी निजी अस्पताल में मांगा जा रहा है या प्राप्त किया जा रहा है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। डीएके केवल दिल्ली सरकार, केंद्र सरकार, एम्स, राज्य सरकार के स्वायत्त संस्थानों या स्थानीय निकायों द्वारा संचालित सरकारी अस्पतालों में उपचार को कवर करता है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Ensure treatment is at an eligible Government Hospital before applying. Verify with the Patient Welfare Cell if unsure whether a specific hospital qualifies under DAK.
examples: Patient seeking treatment at a private hospital or nursing home
Estimate Certificate issued by a private hospital doctor
Treatment received at a private hospital with referral from a government hospital (private hospital treatment still not covered)

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected and legal action initiated if the applicant makes any false statement in the application form, undertaking, or affidavit. Making false statements is punishable under relevant Acts and laws.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक आवेदन पत्र, वचनबद्धता, या शपथ पत्र में कोई झूठा बयान देता है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा और कानूनी कार्रवाई शुरू की जाएगी। झूठे बयान देना संबंधित अधिनियमों और कानूनों के तहत दंडनीय है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Provide only truthful and accurate information in all parts of the application. Disclose all sources of income, prior assistance received, and any other material facts truthfully.
examples: Misrepresenting family income to qualify under the ₹3 lakh income limit
Concealing prior financial assistance received from DAK or Delhi Arogya Nidhi
Falsely declaring non-employment in Central/State Government or Local Body
Providing forged or tampered documents

rejection_rules.description: Application will not be processed if submitted with incomplete documents. All required documents including original Estimate Certificate, original Income Certificate, attested photographs, domicile proof, food security card, treatment records, OPD slip, and signed affidavit with witnesses must be submitted together.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि अधूरे दस्तावेजों के साथ आवेदन जमा किया जाता है तो आवेदन पर कार्रवाई नहीं की जाएगी। मूल अनुमानित व्यय प्रमाण पत्र, मूल आय प्रमाण पत्र, सत्यापित फोटो, अधिवास प्रमाण, खाद्य सुरक्षा कार्ड, उपचार रिकॉर्ड, ओपीडी पर्ची, और गवाहों के साथ हस्ताक्षरित शपथ पत्र सभी एक साथ जमा करने होंगे।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Use the applicant checklist before visiting the Patient Welfare Cell: (1) Food Security Card photocopy + original, (2) Domicile proof, (3) Birth Certificate (if minor) + parent's proof, (4) Original Estimate Certificate with all 3 signatures + seal, (5) 2 attested photos, (6) Treatment records photocopies, (7) OPD slip, (8) Original Income Certificate, (9) Completed Application Form, (10) Signed Affidavit with witnesses, (11) Prior assistance details if any.
examples: Estimate Certificate missing Medical Superintendent's signature or official seal
Only one photograph submitted instead of two
Original income certificate not provided (only photocopy submitted)
Affidavit submitted without witness signatures
Missing domicile proof or food security card

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the applicant or their parents are employees of the Central Government, State Government, or a Local Body. This is explicitly declared in the application form.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि आवेदक या उनके माता-पिता केंद्र सरकार, राज्य सरकार, या स्थानीय निकाय के कर्मचारी हैं तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। यह आवेदन पत्र में स्पष्ट रूप से घोषित किया जाता है।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: DAK is exclusively for non-government employees. Government employees (Central, State, or Local Body) and their children are not eligible. They should explore CGHS/State health schemes available to government employees.
examples: Applicant is a Central Government employee with CGHS coverage
Applicant's parent is a State Government employee
Applicant works in a Municipal Corporation (Local Body)
Retired government employee concealing previous employment status

rejection_rules.description: Application will be rejected if the patient's family annual income exceeds ₹3,00,000 (Three Lakhs). Income certificate must show total family income from all sources within this limit.
rejection_rules.description_hindi: यदि रोगी के परिवार की वार्षिक आय ₹3,00,000 (तीन लाख) से अधिक है तो आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिया जाएगा। आय प्रमाण पत्र में सभी स्रोतों से कुल पारिवारिक आय इस सीमा के भीतर दिखानी होगी।
rejection_rules.prevention_tip: Ensure the income certificate obtained from SDM/authorized officer accurately reflects total family income from all sources. If family income exceeds ₹3 lakh annually, the patient is not eligible for DAK assistance.
examples: Family annual income is ₹3,50,000 which exceeds the ₹3 lakh limit
Income certificate shows monthly income exceeding ₹25,000
Undisclosed income sources discovered during verification that push total above the limit
